_metadata.description: Other/inactive ingredients database for supplement excipients and additives

other_ingredients.notes: Natural soluble fiber from Acacia senegal trees (E414). FDA GRAS. Prebiotic properties — fermented by colonic Bifidobacteria and Lactobacilli to produce short-chain fatty acids. Excellent safety profile with no ADI restriction (JECFA). Also functions as natural binder and stabilizer in tablets.

Acetic acid (CH3COOH) — primary component of vinegar (4-8%). Used as pH adjuster and preservative in supplement formulations. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1005). Antimicrobial at low pH; inhibits pathogenic bacteria. No ADI restriction (JECFA).

Natural thickening and gelling agent derived from red algae. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) and commonly used as a vegan alternative to gelatin. May provide beneficial dietary fiber. Excellent clean label choice.

Agave-derived inulin-type fructan fiber used as prebiotic filler in supplement formulations. Fermented by colonic Bifidobacteria to produce short-chain fatty acids (butyrate, propionate). GRAS. 5-10g/day supports gut microbiome diversity.
Used as extraction solvent or preservative. Generally evaporates during processing. GRAS approved.
Rare natural sugar with 70% sweetness of sucrose but minimal calories. Does not affect blood sugar. FDA recognized as GRAS.
Prebiotic oligosaccharide carbohydrate used for gut-support formulations; mapped as non-scorable identity pending dedicated efficacy scoring policy.
Inorganic salt used as buffering agent and pH adjuster in supplement formulations. GRAS status.

Ammonium molybdate [(NH4)6Mo7O24] — inorganic molybdenum source used as trace mineral supplement. Cofactor for xanthine oxidase, sulfite oxidase, and aldehyde oxidase. FDA-permitted nutrient. UMLS C0002596.
Natural yellow/orange colorant from annatto seeds. GRAS status. Clean label color.
Natural purple/red pigment from berries and fruits. Antioxidant properties. Clean label color. GRAS approved.
Apple cider vinegar used in gummies for digestive health claims. Contains acetic acid. GRAS approved.
Concentrated apple puree used for flavor and natural sweetness. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.

Water-based polymer coating system applied to supplement tablets for moisture protection, taste masking, and improved swallowability. Typically composed of HPMC or PVA in aqueous dispersion. Inert and non-absorbed.
Branded blend descriptor added for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable until ingredient-level decomposition.
Natural red/pink coloring from beetroot juice. Rich in betalains, provides antioxidant benefits. GRAS approved.
common_uses: clean label alternative to artificial colors

other_ingredients.notes: Various berry purees used for flavor and color. Clean label ingredients. GRAS approved.
Natural purple/red colorant from black carrot juice, rich in anthocyanins. Popular in gummies as a clean label alternative to artificial colors. GRAS approved.
Natural flavoring derived from black cherry extracts and compounds. Provides authentic black cherry taste to supplement formulations.
Black pepper extract containing piperine. Enhances absorption of other nutrients. GRAS approved.

Nutrient-rich byproduct of sugar cane refining (third boiling). Contains iron (4.7mg/tbsp), calcium, potassium, magnesium, and B6. Used as natural sweetener and mineral source in supplement formulations. GRAS food ingredient.
Brown seaweed traditionally used for thyroid support due to iodine content. GRAS approved.
Natural blue/purple colorant from blueberry juice. Rich in anthocyanins. Clean label. GRAS approved.

Plant-derived wax (carnauba, candelilla, or rice bran wax) used as tablet polish and moisture barrier coating. FDA GRAS. Inert — passes through GI tract unabsorbed. Provides glossy finish and protects against humidity.
Phytochemical constituent descriptor captured for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable unless elevated to dedicated IQM entry.

Calcium salt of casein (milk protein) used as protein source and tablet binder. Contains ~90% protein with complete amino acid profile. Dairy allergen — must declare per FALCPA. GRAS food ingredient. Slow-digesting protein matrix.

Calcium chloride (CaCl2) — mineral salt used as firming agent, electrolyte source, and processing aid in supplement manufacturing. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1193). Hygroscopic; also serves as desiccant in packaging.

Calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2, slaked lime) — strong alkaline pH adjuster used in small amounts in food and supplement processing. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1205). Used in traditional nixtamalization and water treatment.
Natural calcium salt that's easily absorbed. Often preferred over calcium carbonate for bioavailability. Generally well-tolerated.
FDA GRAS-approved anticaking agent, emulsifier, and lubricant. Listed in FDA regulations 21 CFR 172.863 and 175.300. CAS Registry Number: 542-42-7.
Non-psychoactive cannabinoid from hemp. Precursor cannabinoid. Regulatory status similar to CBD.
Mildly psychoactive cannabinoid from hemp. Often marketed for sleep. Regulatory status varies.
Natural emulsifier from canola/rapeseed. Alternative to soy lecithin. GRAS approved.

Natural orange/yellow colorant from Daucus carota (carrot) extract, rich in beta-carotene and alpha-carotene. FDA exempt from certification (21 CFR 73). Clean-label alternative to synthetic FD&C dyes. Also provides provitamin A activity.
Natural orange colorant from carrot juice. Rich in beta-carotene. Clean label. GRAS approved.
Protein hydrolysate descriptor added for mapping coverage; classify as non-scorable unless curated into IQM.
Natural plant fiber used as a bulking agent and source of dietary fiber. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Excellent clean label choice for adding fiber content.

Matricaria chamomilla (German chamomile) extract containing apigenin, bisabolol, and chamazulene. Used for calming and sleep support in supplement formulations. GRAS flavoring. Monographed by EMA (HMPC/281642/2013). Cross-reactive with ragweed allergens.
Combination of natural cherry extracts and artificial cherry compounds to provide cherry taste. Natural extracts from real cherries combined with synthetic compounds that replicate cherry flavor.
Chicory-derived prebiotic fiber used for sweetness reduction and gut health support.
Chili pepper containing capsaicin. Used for metabolism and circulation. GRAS approved.

Sodium copper chlorophyllin (E141ii) — water-soluble semisynthetic derivative of chlorophyll used as green colorant in supplements. FDA exempt from certification (21 CFR 73.125). Also studied for internal deodorant properties and aflatoxin binding.

Natural chocolate flavoring derived from Theobroma cacao. Used for taste masking of bitter active ingredients in chewable tablets and gummies. GRAS flavoring (21 CFR 182.10). Contains trace theobromine. No pharmacological effect at flavoring levels.

Natural preservative and pH adjuster. While naturally found in citrus, commercial form is typically derived from fermented sources. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Clean label preservative option.

Essential oil from citrus peel (Citrus spp.) containing limonene, linalool, and citral. GRAS flavoring (21 CFR 182.20). Used for taste enhancement and mild antimicrobial activity. Phototoxic compounds (bergapten) removed in supplement-grade oils.
Natural fiber from citrus fruits used as bulking agent and prebiotic. Clean label alternative to synthetic fibers.
Natural fat extracted from cocoa beans. Used as a carrier for fat-soluble ingredients and to improve texture in supplements. Melts at body temperature.
Various forms of coconut-derived ingredients used as carriers or fillers for fat-soluble ingredients. Generally safe, well-tolerated, and offer clean label appeal.

Dried coconut (Cocos nucifera) in granulated form, used for flavor, texture, and as medium-chain fatty acid source (lauric acid ~47%). GRAS food ingredient. Contains ~65% total fat. Used as carrier in powder supplement formulations.
Broken down collagen protein for better absorption. Popular for skin, hair, nail, and joint health. GRAS approved.
Composite mineral descriptor added for mapping coverage; requires explicit mineral breakdown for IQM scoring.
Cranberry concentrate used for urinary tract health. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.

Cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) seed and hull fiber used as plant-based filler and carrier in supplement formulations. Contains proanthocyanidins (PACs). Provides dietary fiber (~4g/10g) and antioxidant polyphenols.
Phytochemical constituent descriptor captured for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable unless elevated to dedicated IQM entry.

Deionized/distilled water — purified water with minerals and ions removed via deionization or distillation. USP-grade solvent used in liquid supplement formulations, reconstitution, and manufacturing. Meets USP <1231> specifications.

Natural aldopentose sugar from wood/plant fiber. FDA GRAS. Used as sweetener/excipient (~40-70% sweetness of sucrose, ~2.4 cal/g). Not a bioactive supplement ingredient — no human clinical trials support therapeutic use.
Proprietary blend of natural sweeteners typically including stevia, monk fruit, and other natural sources. Clean label alternative to artificial sweeteners.

aliases: Echinacea purpurea (aerial and root parts) 4:1 extract
other_ingredients.notes: Immune-supporting herb extract. Popular for cold and flu support. GRAS approved.

Egg white (albumin) protein — complete protein source (3.6g protein per large egg white, PDCAAS 1.0). Used as binder and film former in supplement tablets. Egg allergen — must declare per FALCPA. Contains ovalbumin, ovotransferrin, ovomucoid.
Elderberry extract used for immune support. Popular cold and flu remedy. GRAS approved.
Natural colorant from elderberry juice. Rich in anthocyanins. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.
Phytochemical constituent descriptor captured for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable unless elevated to dedicated IQM entry.
Cellulose derivative used as coating agent and controlled release matrix. Provides moisture barrier and taste masking. GRAS approved with excellent safety profile.

Ethyl maltol (2-ethyl-3-hydroxy-4H-pyran-4-one) — synthetic flavor enhancer 4-6× more potent than maltol. GRAS flavoring (FEMA 3487). Used at ppm levels for sweetness enhancement and flavor rounding in chewable/gummy supplements.

Brilliant Blue FCF (FD&C Blue No. 1, E133) — synthetic triarylmethane dye. FDA-certified color additive (21 CFR 74.101). ADI 6 mg/kg bw/day (EFSA, 2010). Used for tablet/capsule identification. Low oral bioavailability (~5%).

Quinoline Yellow (D&C Yellow No. 10, E104) — synthetic quinoline dye used for tablet/capsule coloring. FDA-approved for drugs and cosmetics. ADI 0.5 mg/kg bw/day (EFSA, 2009). Not permitted in food in some jurisdictions.
Prebiotic fiber composed of short fructose chains. FDA GRAS. Used as prebiotic and mild sweetener.
Natural fruit and vegetable juices used for flavor, color, and sweetness. Clean label ingredient.
Dehydrated and powdered plants used for natural color and flavor. Generally safe and may provide some antioxidant benefits. Premium clean-label alternative to artificial dyes.

aliases: containing one or more of the following:
other_ingredients.notes: Generic coloring term. May indicate natural or artificial colors - specific identification recommended. Generally safe but vague labeling.
Ginger root extract used for digestive and anti-inflammatory benefits. GRAS approved.

Natural ginger (Zingiber officinale) flavoring containing gingerols and shogaols. GRAS flavoring (21 CFR 182.10). Used for taste masking and flavor enhancement in liquid and chewable supplement formulations. Mild sialagogue effect.
Natural sugar derived from corn or wheat starch. Provides quick energy and acts as a binding agent. GRAS approved by FDA.

Glycerin esters of fatty acids (E472a-f) — food-grade emulsifiers produced by esterification of glycerol with food-grade fatty acids. GRAS. Used for emulsification, texture modification, and foam control in supplement formulations.

Emulsifier derived from glycerin and fatty acids (mono/diglycerides, E471). GRAS (21 CFR 184.1505). Used for emulsification, crystal modification, and dough conditioning in supplement and food matrices. ADI: not specified (JECFA).

Glyceryl caprylate/caprate — medium-chain glyceride ester used as co-emulsifier and solubilizer in supplement formulations. Derived from coconut/palm kernel oil fractions. GRAS. Enhances dispersion of lipophilic active ingredients.

Natural sweetener derived from licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra). Approximately 30-50x sweeter than sugar. Used in small amounts as flavor enhancer. MAG form commonly used in supplements. May raise blood pressure in sensitive individuals with excessive use.

Grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) fiber — plant-derived dietary fiber used as filler, carrier, and bulking agent in supplement tablets and capsules. Contains pectin and cellulose. GRAS food ingredient. Provides ~3g fiber per 10g.
Natural colorant from grape juice. Rich in anthocyanins. Clean label alternative to artificial colors. GRAS approved.

aliases: Full Spectrum Hemp Aerial Parts Extract
organic broad-spectrum hemp aerial part extract
other_ingredients.notes: Hemp extract containing various phytocannabinoids. Legal hemp contains <0.3% THC. Regulatory status varies by state.
Branded ingredient descriptor added for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable until ingredient-level decomposition.
Coconut oil that has been hydrogenated for stability. Used as carrier and for texture. Less clean label than unhydrogenated.

Hypromellose phthalate (HPMCP) — cellulose-derived enteric coating polymer that resists gastric acid (pH <5.0) and dissolves in intestinal pH (>5.5). Used for acid-sensitive ingredients and targeted intestinal release. NF/EP excipient.
Prebiotic fiber commonly derived from chicory root. Supports gut health and texture in gummies.
Red algae used for mineral content and as a thickening agent. Popular in wellness products. GRAS approved.
Prebiotic fiber syrup used for texture and mild sweetness. Generally well-tolerated and used as a fiber source.
Natural fruit juice concentrates used for color and flavor. Clean label ingredients. GRAS approved.

Kaolin (hydrated aluminum silicate, E559) — white clay mineral used as filler, adsorbent, and anti-caking agent in tablets. FDA GRAS. Also used as tablet coating opacifier. Not systemically absorbed; passes through GI tract intact.
Protein for hair, skin, and nail health. Derived from wool or feathers. GRAS approved.
Sugar alcohol derived from lactose. Lower glycemic impact than sugar, commonly used in diabetic foods. GRAS approved.
Natural emulsifier derived from sunflowers. Often preferred over soy lecithin due to non-GMO and allergen concerns. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Premium clean label emulsifier.

Melissa officinalis (lemon balm) extract containing rosmarinic acid and flavonoids. Used for calming, cognitive support, and sleep aid in supplement formulations. EMA-monographed (HMPC/196745/2012). GRAS flavoring. GABAergic mechanism proposed.
Natural flavoring derived from lemon extracts and compounds. Provides authentic lemon taste to supplement formulations.
Branded blend descriptor added for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable until ingredient-level decomposition.

Magnesium borate mineral salt used as buffering/processing aid in supplement manufacturing. Provides trace boron and magnesium. Not a primary nutrient source at excipient levels. Limited toxicological data at low doses.
Magnesium phosphate used as pH buffering agent and anticaking agent in supplements.

Widely used tablet and capsule lubricant. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Primary considerations are source (plant vs animal) and vegan/halal/kosher suitability. No strong evidence of reduced nutrient absorption at typical use levels.
Inorganic compound used as antacid and pharmaceutical excipient. FDA-listed inactive ingredient for oral tablets. Acts as adsorbent and anti-caking agent. GRAS for food contact use.
Natural fruit acid found in apples and other fruits. Used for tartness and as a natural preservative. Well-tolerated and beneficial for energy metabolism.
Natural disaccharide sugar derived from malted grains. Less sweet than sucrose, commonly used in food products. GRAS approved.
Natural sweetener from malted grains. Less refined than corn syrup. Used in gummies for sweetness and texture. GRAS approved.

Natural mandarin (Citrus reticulata) peel flavoring containing limonene and methyl N-methylanthranilate. GRAS flavoring (21 CFR 182.20). Used for citrus taste enhancement in chewable and liquid supplement formulations.
Mango puree used for flavor and natural sweetness. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.
Natural sweeteners derived from maple sap or sugar cane. Contain minerals and trace nutrients.
Branded multi-botanical blend name captured for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable until decomposed.
Plant-derived cellulose used as bulking agent and anticaking agent. Insoluble fiber. GRAS approved.

aliases: organic Aronia Berry (Sorbaronia mitschurinii) juice concentrate
other_ingredients.notes: Various fruit and vegetable juice concentrates used for natural color and flavor. Clean label ingredients. GRAS approved.

Category-level entry for natural flavoring agents not individually mapped (e.g., raspberry, cherry vanilla, tutti frutti). All GRAS under 21 CFR 182.10. Derived from botanical or animal sources per 21 CFR 101.22.

Natural mixed berry flavoring (strawberry, blueberry, raspberry) used for taste masking and flavor enhancement in chewable tablets and gummies. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Anthocyanin-derived. No pharmacological activity at flavoring levels.
Chemically or physically modified starch used as thickener and stabilizer. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Common in food and supplements.

Monk fruit (Siraitia grosvenorii, also known as lo han guo/luo han guo) extract. FDA GRAS (GRN 301, 795). Natural zero-calorie sweetener 150-300× sweeter than sucrose. Active compounds are mogrosides (triterpenoid glycosides), primarily mogroside V. No ADI restriction per JECFA.

aliases: mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids
other_ingredients.notes: Common emulsifiers derived from natural fats and oils. Help blend oil and water-based ingredients. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS).
Branded blend descriptor added for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable until ingredient-level decomposition.

aliases: Lion's Mane Mushroom Fruiting Body Extract
other_ingredients.notes: Functional mushroom extracts used for various health benefits. Popular for cognitive and immune support. GRAS approved.
Mushroom mycelium grown on brown rice substrate. Used as carrier/base for medicinal mushroom supplements.

Natural apple (Malus domestica) flavoring containing ethyl 2-methylbutyrate and hexyl acetate. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Used for taste masking in chewable supplements. No pharmacological effect at flavoring concentrations.
Natural flavoring derived from berry extracts and essential oils. GRAS approved.

Natural blueberry (Vaccinium corymbosum) flavoring derived from fruit extracts. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Contains anthocyanin pigments. Used for taste masking and visual appeal in gummy and chewable supplement formulations.

Natural cinnamon (Cinnamomum spp.) flavoring containing cinnamaldehyde. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Used for taste masking in chewable/liquid supplements. No blood sugar effects at trace flavoring levels (unlike therapeutic doses).
Natural coloring agents derived from plant, mineral, or other natural sources. Provides color without synthetic dyes.

aliases: fruit and vegetable juice color concentrates
other_ingredients.notes: Natural colorants derived from plants, fruits, and vegetables. Premium clean label alternative to synthetic dyes with additional antioxidant benefits.
common_uses: clean label alternative to artificial colors

aliases: natural fruit flavors with other natural flavors
organic tropical flavor and other natural flavors
other_ingredients.notes: Flavoring substances derived from plant or animal sources. While generally recognized as safe, the term can encompass hundreds of compounds. Must be derived from natural sources per FDA regulations. Good clean label choice.

Catch-all entry for natural flavoring agents derived from plant/animal sources per 21 CFR 101.22. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Includes hundreds of FEMA-listed flavoring substances used at sub-therapeutic levels for taste optimization.

Natural grape (Vitis vinifera) flavoring containing methyl anthranilate and ethyl acetate. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Used for taste masking in chewable tablets and liquid supplements. Derived from grape juice/essence.
Natural gums used as thickeners, binders, and stabilizers. Generally safe and can be a source of dietary fiber. May cause bloating or gas in some individuals. Good clean label choice.

Natural mint (Mentha spp.) flavoring containing menthol (30-50%), menthone, and menthyl acetate. GRAS (21 CFR 182.20). Used for cooling sensation and taste masking in oral supplement formulations. Mild carminative.

Natural orange (Citrus sinensis) flavoring containing limonene (>90%), octanal, and decanal. GRAS (21 CFR 182.20). Most common citrus flavoring in supplement gummies and chewables. No pharmacological activity at flavor levels.
Natural flavoring derived from citrus sources including orange and tangerine. FEMA GRAS approved as citrus-derived natural flavor complex. FDA 21 CFR Part 172 compliant.
Antioxidants used to prevent spoilage, such as fat-soluble Vitamin C (ascorbyl palmitate) or Vitamin E (tocopherols). Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) and beneficial. Premium clean label choice.

Generic label descriptor for non-caloric or low-caloric natural sweeteners (stevia, monk fruit, erythritol). Identity mapping for compliance; specific sweetener entries exist separately. GRAS category.

Premium natural flavoring from vanilla beans. Includes both natural vanillin compounds and full vanilla extracts. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). Highly desirable clean label ingredient with excellent consumer perception.

Natural watermelon (Citrullus lanatus) flavoring. GRAS (21 CFR 182.10). Contains cis-3-hexenal and nonanal. Used for taste masking in chewable and liquid supplement formulations. No pharmacological effects at flavoring doses.
Plant-based materials used for natural coatings and glazing. Provides protective barrier and improves appearance. Vegan-friendly alternative to animal-derived waxes. Excellent clean label choice.
Natural sweetener derived from citrus bioflavonoids. Approximately 1500x sweeter than sugar. Used in very small amounts. E959 in EU. Derived from naringin in citrus peel.
Naturally occurring monounsaturated omega-9 fatty acid. Used as emulsifier and processing aid. Heart-healthy fat. GRAS approved.
Crystallized orange flavoring, typically made from concentrated natural orange extracts or oils. Used for flavor enhancement in lozenges and chewables.

Natural or artificial orange flavoring used for taste masking and consumer palatability in supplement formulations. GRAS (21 CFR 182.20). Contains limonene as primary aroma compound. Standard in citrus-flavored gummies.

Natural flavoring derived from orange extracts and compounds, including blood orange varieties. Provides authentic orange taste to supplement formulations. FEMA GRAS approved as citrus-derived natural flavor.
Organic sweetener from sugar cane. Less processed than refined sugar, retains some minerals.
Organic form of evaporated cane juice. Less processed than refined sugar, retains some minerals. Clean label natural sweetener option.

PHGG — enzymatically hydrolyzed galactomannan from Cyamopsis tetragonoloba. Low-viscosity soluble prebiotic fiber (Sunfiber brand). FDA GRAS (GRN 61). Fermented by colonic bacteria to butyrate. Studied for IBS-D (doi:10.1016/j.nut.2006.01.012).
Calming herb extract used for relaxation, anxiety, and sleep support. GRAS approved.
Natural flavoring derived from peach extracts and compounds. Provides authentic peach taste to supplement formulations.
Natural fiber from fruits, excellent for gelling and thickening. Provides additional health benefits including cholesterol support.

Enzymatic protein hydrolysate preparation (typically from casein or soy) used as nitrogen source in probiotic fermentation media and as binder in tablet formulations. USP/NF excipient. Contains free amino acids and small peptides.

Phosphoric acid (H3PO4, E338) — mineral acid used for pH adjustment and buffering in liquid supplement formulations. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 182.1073). ADI 70 mg/kg bw/day (JECFA). Concerns at high chronic intake re calcium metabolism.
Natural flavoring derived from pineapple extracts and compounds. Provides authentic pineapple taste to supplement formulations.

Methacrylic acid-methyl methacrylate copolymer (Eudragit L/S) — enteric coating that dissolves at pH >5.5-7.0 for targeted intestinal release. USP/NF monographed. Used for acid-labile ingredients (enzymes, probiotics). Not systemically absorbed.
Pomegranate juice used for color, flavor, and antioxidant benefits. Clean label. GRAS approved.

Potassium chelated with amino acids (typically glycine) for improved mineral absorption. Used as potassium source in supplement formulations where chelated mineral forms are preferred. GRAS. Contains ~14-20% elemental potassium.
Natural potassium salt with good bioavailability. Often used in electrolyte formulations and as a natural preservative.
Natural purple colorant from various berries, rich in anthocyanins. Clean label alternative to artificial colors. GRAS approved.
Natural purple colorant from purple carrot juice, rich in anthocyanins. Clean label natural color alternative. GRAS approved.

Polyvinylpyrrolidone (povidone, E1201) — synthetic water-soluble polymer used as binder, film former, and solubilizer in tablet manufacturing. USP/NF monographed (K-values 12-90). Not systemically absorbed. FDA IIG listed.

Quillaja saponaria bark extract (E999) — natural saponin-rich emulsifier and foaming agent. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 172.510). Used in liquid supplement formulations for solubilization of lipophilic compounds. Adjuvant properties studied in vaccines.
Natural raspberry fruit powder providing flavor and nutrients. Clean label ingredient from real fruit.
Natural, hypoallergenic ingredient used as a flow agent or lubricant. Serving as a clean-label alternative to magnesium stearate. Excellent premium clean label choice.
Rice-based mineral carrier/chelating agent used for improved mineral absorption in supplements.
Natural rice-derived extract used as anticaking and flow agent. Clean label alternative to synthetic flow agents.

Extract from various marine algae (kelp, dulse, wakame). Source of iodine, fucoidan, and trace minerals. Used as thickener, mineral source, and functional ingredient in supplement formulations. Variable iodine content requires standardization.
Natural silica source from bamboo, preferred over synthetic silicon dioxide. Provides natural flow properties with additional bioactive compounds.

Likely label misspelling of silicon dioxide (SiO2, E551). Anti-caking agent and tablet glidant. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 172.480, ≤2% of food weight). Amorphous form used in supplements; not systemically absorbed. Entry preserved for label matching.
Dried milk with fat removed. Contains milk allergen. Used as protein source and filler in some supplements.

Sodium bisulfate (NaHSO4, E514ii) — acidifying agent used for pH control in food and supplement applications. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1721). Strong acid; used at low concentrations. May cause sulfite sensitivity in susceptible individuals.
Natural pH regulator and buffer commonly used in food and supplements. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) with a long history of use. Clean label choice for pH adjustment.
Alkalizing agent and pH regulator. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) when used as a food additive. Common processing aid.

FDA GRAS-approved buffer and acidity regulator widely used safely in foods and supplements. Helps stabilize pH and acts as natural preservative. Consolidates two previous duplicate entries (Sodium Citrate and Trisodium Citrate - they are the same compound). Only consideration is moderate sodium content for sodium-restricted diets.
Strong base used as pH adjuster in supplement manufacturing. FDA GRAS for food use.
Sodium salt of lactic acid used as humectant and preservative. Helps maintain moisture in gummies. GRAS approved.

Sodium malate (E350) — sodium salt of L-malic acid used as acidity regulator and flavor enhancer in supplement formulations. GRAS. Provides tart/sour taste. Good buffering capacity in pH 3-5 range. ADI: not specified (JECFA).

Rochelle salt (E337) — double tartrate salt used as acidity regulator, leavening agent, and emulsifier. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1804). Also known as Seignette's salt. Used in effervescent supplement formulations.
Clean-label tablet lubricant and disintegrant. Used as alternative to magnesium stearate. Generally recognized as safe.
Inorganic salt used as processing aid and drying agent in supplement manufacturing. Also known as Glauber's salt.
Label/form-factor descriptor captured for mapping suppression; not an ingredient.

Soluble corn fiber (SCF) — resistant dextrin from corn starch. FDA GRAS (GRN 449, Tate & Lyle). Prebiotic fermentation increases Bifidobacteria and butyrate. Low glycemic. Well-tolerated at 12g/day (doi:10.1017/S0007114512001560).
Sugar alcohol used as sweetener and humectant. Generally safe but may cause digestive issues in large amounts.
Soy-derived protein hydrolysate used as fermentation growth medium for probiotic cultures.
Natural flavoring derived from spearmint extracts and compounds. Provides authentic spearmint taste to supplement formulations.
Chemically modified starch for improved stability and texture. FDA approved food additive.
Plant oil high in GLA (gamma-linolenic acid). Used for skin and hormonal health. GRAS approved.
Fatty acid used as excipient and lubricant. GRAS with long history of safe use. Source (plant vs animal) affects vegan suitability.

Natural, calorie-free sweetener from the Stevia rebaudiana plant. FDA GRAS status applies to HIGH-PURITY extracts (≥95% steviol glycosides) including rebaudioside A and stevioside. Whole stevia leaves and crude extracts are NOT FDA GRAS approved. Includes branded forms like BetterStevia, Truvia, and PureVia. Clean label alternative to artificial sweeteners.
Strawberry puree used for flavor and color. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.
Unrefined whole cane sugar retaining natural molasses and minerals. Less processed than white sugar. GRAS approved.
Emulsifier derived from sucrose and fatty acids. Used in gummies for texture. GRAS approved.
Natural sugars used for sweetening and palatability. Generally recognized as safe but should be consumed in moderation.

Sunfiber (Taiyo International) — branded partially hydrolyzed guar gum (PHGG) prebiotic fiber. FDA GRAS (GRN 61). Low viscosity, water-soluble. Clinically studied for IBS symptoms and regularity. 5-7g/day effective dose.

Sunfiber AG — agricultural/food-grade variant of Sunfiber PHGG prebiotic fiber (Taiyo International). Same base material as standard Sunfiber but formulated for food/supplement manufacturing applications. FDA GRAS.
Esterified sunflower oil used as emulsifier and coating agent in supplement tablets.
Natural oil from sunflower seeds used as carrier for fat-soluble vitamins. Clean label ingredient. GRAS approved.
Phytochemical constituent descriptor captured for mapping coverage; treat as non-scorable unless elevated to dedicated IQM entry.
Soluble fiber derived from tapioca used for texture and sugar reduction in gummies.
Aromatic compounds from plants. Used for flavor and entourage effect in hemp products. GRAS approved.

Natural red/orange colorant from Solanum lycopersicum (tomato) extract, rich in lycopene. FDA exempt from certification (21 CFR 73). Clean-label alternative to synthetic red dyes. Lycopene provides additional antioxidant properties.
Natural disaccharide sugar found in plants, fungi, and insects. Less sweet than sucrose, used for stability and moisture retention. GRAS approved.

Trimagnesium dicitrate — magnesium salt of citric acid providing ~16% elemental magnesium. Used as magnesium source and buffering agent in supplement formulations. Well-absorbed form. FDA GRAS. Common in powder supplements.

Trisodium citrate dihydrate (E331iii) — sodium salt of citric acid used as buffering agent, emulsifier, and acidity regulator. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1792). Same compound as sodium citrate. Standard pharmaceutical/food excipient.

Plant-derived cellulose (methylcellulose or HPMC) applied as tablet coating for moisture protection, taste masking, and improved swallowability. FDA GRAS. Inert — not systemically absorbed. Standard clean-label coating material.
Natural colorants derived from vegetables and fruits. Clean label alternative to artificial colors. GRAS approved.

Plant-derived mono- and diglycerides (E471) used as emulsifiers and texture modifiers in supplement formulations. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 184.1505). Produced from vegetable oils (palm, soy, canola). ADI: not specified (JECFA).

Plant-derived glycerol (1,2,3-propanetriol, E422) used as humectant, solvent, and sweetener in supplement formulations. FDA GRAS. Essential for gummy texture and shelf life. Acts as carrier for flavors and herbal extracts. ADI: not specified (JECFA — acceptable for food use).

Plant-derived lubricants (vegetable magnesium stearate, stearic acid, or MCT oil) used to prevent tablet sticking to manufacturing equipment. FDA GRAS. Reduce friction during compression. Typically ≤1% of tablet weight.
Plant-derived fatty acids and stearates used as natural lubricants and flow agents in manufacturing. Cleaner alternative to mineral-derived stearates. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS).

Vegetable-sourced magnesium silicate (talc analog, E553a) — anti-caking agent and tablet glidant. FDA GRAS. Improves powder flow during manufacturing. Not systemically absorbed. Distinct from asbestos-contaminated talc.

Palatinose (isomaltulose, E953) — naturally occurring disaccharide (glucose-fructose linked α-1,6). Low glycemic index (GI 32 vs sucrose 65). FDA GRAS (GRN 184). Slow-release energy. Found in honey and sugarcane.
Branded milk-protein peptide complex (alpha-s1 casein hydrolysate). Mapped as identity-only to avoid form-score inflation.
Astragalus-derived triterpenoid. Identity-only mapping pending evidence-gated scoring policy.

aliases: panax notoginseng and rosa roxburghii blend
other_ingredients.notes: Branded botanical complex (Panax notoginseng + Rosa roxburghii fractions). Identity-only mapping; not treated as product-level proprietary blend container by default.
Identity-only mapping for dried yeast fermentate complexes (including branded EpiCor-style labels).

Soynatto — branded fermented whole soybean food product (natto-type). Contains nattokinase and vitamin K2 (MK-7). Identity mapping for fermented soy product labels. Soy allergen — must declare per FALCPA.

Paeoniflorin — monoterpene glycoside marker compound from Paeonia lactiflora (white peony root). Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory. Used as quality control marker for peony extract standardization in supplement labels.

Isovitexin (apigenin-6-C-glucoside) — C-glycosyl flavonoid marker compound found in passion flower, bamboo leaves, and hawthorn. Antioxidant and anxiolytic properties. Used as standardization marker on supplement labels.

Gamma-butyrobetaine HCl (GBB) — carnitine biosynthesis precursor. Substrate for gamma-butyrobetaine hydroxylase. Used in sports supplements as L-carnitine precursor. Identity mapping for branded ingredient labels (GBB-EXT).
Identity-only mapping for garlic marker-compound labels; scoring policy pending evidence/form curation.
Identity-only mapping for abbreviated performance-label term GKG; mapped conservatively pending explicit chemistry confirmation.
Identity-only mapping for A-KIC label abbreviation (alpha-ketoisocaproic-acid shorthand).
Identity-only mapping for NMDA-labeled ingredient rows pending explicit scoring policy.
Identity-only mapping for branded blend component labels (Cyplexinol/Boswellia-associated marketing term).
ATPro — branded adenosine triphosphate (ATP) support complex. Identity mapping for proprietary peak-ATP/energy-complex label tokens. Specific composition varies by manufacturer formulation.

Enzopharm Plus — branded enzyme complex formulation. Identity mapping for proprietary digestive/systemic enzyme blend label text. Contains protease, lipase, amylase, and specialty enzymes per manufacturer specification.

DNF-10 — branded yeast-hydrolysate ingredient (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) used in sleep and relaxation supplements. Contains gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) precursors. Identity mapping for branded label tokens.

FRAC — branded antioxidant/ORAC (Oxygen Radical Absorbance Capacity) complex descriptor. Identity mapping for proprietary antioxidant blend naming. Specific composition varies by manufacturer formulation.
Identity-only mapping for branded ingredient token EP107 pending compositional disclosure.
Identity-only mapping for peat-derived extract rows (often in elevATP formulations).
Identity-only mapping for milk-casein tripeptide labels (IPP/VPP) pending dedicated scoring policy.

Cyplexinol — branded bone-protein complex containing collagen peptides and growth factors. Identity mapping for proprietary joint/bone support label tokens. Clinical studies in bone mineral density support.
Identity-only mapping for Mitopure-branded Urolithin A rows until dedicated IQM parent/form policy is finalized.
Identity-only mapping for Phytopin trademark tokens to avoid unmapped branded wrapper rows where phytosterol actives are separately listed.
Identity-only mapping for PhytoSure certification/brand wrapper rows where quercetin active is separately represented.
Standardization marker descriptor (typically for ginkgo extracts); identity-only mapping to avoid double-counting marker rows.
Standardization marker descriptor for resveratrol-containing extracts; identity-only mapping.
Descriptor marker for bile extract potency; identity-only mapping pending dedicated policy.

Bovine thyroid glandular tissue — used in traditional glandular therapy for thyroid support. Contains desiccated thyroid hormones (T3/T4 at trace levels). Identity mapping for glandular descriptor label rows.
Legacy naming descriptor (commonly S-methylmethionine contexts); identity-only mapping pending explicit form policy.

Transport C-Plus — branded enhanced vitamin C delivery complex. Identity mapping for proprietary ascorbic acid transport system label tokens. Composition varies by manufacturer (typically liposomal or buffered form).

Univestin — branded botanical complex (Scutellaria baicalensis + Acacia catechu) by Unigen Inc. Standardized to baicalin and catechins. Studied for joint comfort (doi:10.1186/1472-6882-12-8). Identity mapping for label tokens.
Polyphenol class marker term; identity-only mapping to avoid marker row unmapped drift.

Sumalate — branded mineral chelate form (Albion Minerals) using malic acid chelation. Identity mapping for proprietary mineral chelate label tokens. Typically used for iron (ferrous bisglycinate chelate with malate).

Neumentix — branded spearmint (Mentha spicata) extract by Kemin Industries. Standardized to rosmarinic acid (≥14.5%). Studied for working memory and cognitive performance. Identity mapping for label tokens.

NEM (Natural Eggshell Membrane) — branded eggshell membrane ingredient by ESM Technologies. Contains collagen, elastin, glycosaminoglycans (chondroitin, hyaluronic acid). Egg allergen — must declare per FALCPA. Joint support claims.

Mythocondro — branded joint support complex containing chondroitin sulfate from bio-fermentation (non-animal source). Identity mapping for proprietary joint-health label tokens. Vegan chondroitin alternative.

Mobilee — branded hyaluronic acid matrix ingredient (Bioiberica). Contains HA, collagen, and polysaccharides from rooster comb extract. Studied for joint mobility. Identity mapping for proprietary label tokens.

Mirtogenol — branded botanical blend of Pycnogenol (pine bark) + Mirtoselect (bilberry extract) by Horphag/Indena. Studied for intraocular pressure (doi:10.1159/000268121). Identity mapping for label tokens.

Descriptor row for minor cannabinoid profile in hemp/CBD supplement labels (e.g., CBG, CBN, CBC, CBDV). Identity mapping for constituent profile descriptors. Non-discrete ingredient — represents analytical profile data.
Identity-only mapping for MPFF descriptor rows pending dedicated composition policy.

Receptomax — branded controlled-release profile system descriptor. Identity mapping for proprietary time-release technology label text. Specific excipient composition varies by manufacturer formulation.
Syntol — branded probiotic + enzyme digestive cleanse formula name (Arthur Andrew Medical). Identity mapping for proprietary formulation name. Contains protease, cellulase, and probiotic strains.

True Food/SuperPotency/SoyAgen — branded whole-food supplement formula names. Identity mapping for proprietary product naming text. Non-discrete ingredients — represent marketing/formulation branding.

ImmunoLin — branded immunoglobulin concentrate (IgG) from bovine serum (Proliant Health). Contains ≥50% IgG. Studied for GI immune support and pathogen binding. Identity mapping for proprietary label tokens.

L-Arabinose — five-carbon aldose sugar found in plant cell walls. Inhibits intestinal sucrase enzyme, reducing sucrose absorption. GRAS. Studied for postprandial blood glucose modulation (doi:10.1017/S0007114510002060).

Sarcosine (N-methylglycine) — endogenous amino acid derivative. NMDA receptor co-agonist. Studied for cognitive enhancement and adjunct therapy in schizophrenia (doi:10.1016/j.biopsych.2004.09.009). Identity mapping for label tokens.

Rutaecarpine — quinazolinocarboline alkaloid from Evodia rutaecarpa (wu zhu yu). CYP1A2 inducer — accelerates caffeine metabolism. Used in stimulant-containing supplement formulations. Identity mapping for marker rows.
Marketing descriptor for immune support/response claims on supplement labels. Non-discrete ingredient — mapped for identity routing of functional claim text. No specific chemical composition.

Glycosaponins — class of steroidal or triterpenoid saponin glycosides found in various botanicals (fenugreek, ginseng, yucca). Constituent marker label descriptor. Specific identity depends on source plant.

Flavanols — subclass of flavonoids including catechin, epicatechin, and proanthocyanidins. Constituent marker label descriptor. Found in cocoa, green tea, and grapes. Antioxidant and cardiovascular health markers.
Constituent marker row associated with tongkat-ali products; identity-only mapping.
Ergogen XT — branded ergogenic (performance-enhancing) complex descriptor. Identity mapping for proprietary sports supplement formulation naming. Specific composition varies by manufacturer.
Emothion — branded reduced glutathione (L-GSH) ingredient. Identity mapping for proprietary antioxidant label tokens. Specific manufacturing process (fermentation-derived) varies by supplier.
Ecoganic Super Fruits — branded organic/ecologically-sourced fruit blend descriptor. Identity mapping for proprietary blend naming. Non-discrete ingredient — represents marketing/formulation text.

ETAS (Enzyme-Treated Asparagus Extract) — branded asparagus officinalis extract by Amino Up Chemical. Contains hydroxymethylfurfural derivatives. Studied for heat shock protein 70 induction and stress response.

DHQVital — branded dihydroquercetin (taxifolin) ingredient. Potent flavonoid antioxidant from Larix sibirica (Siberian larch). Studied for capillary integrity and anti-inflammatory effects. Identity mapping for label tokens.

Dihydroquercetin 3-rhamnoside (astilbin) — flavanone glycoside found in Smilax glabra (sarsaparilla) and Astilbe thunbergii. Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory marker compound. Used as standardization marker on labels.

Diatomaceous earth (amorphous silica from diatom fossils, E551c) — used as anti-caking agent and filtration aid. FDA GRAS for food-grade amorphous form. Contains 80-90% amorphous silica. Distinct from crystalline form (hazardous).

GBB-GO — branded gamma-butyrobetaine ethyl ester ingredient for sports supplements. Carnitine biosynthesis precursor marketed for enhanced L-carnitine production and thermogenesis. Identity mapping for label tokens.

CogniBoost — branded cognitive support complex descriptor. Identity mapping for proprietary nootropic formulation naming. Specific composition (typically bacopa, phosphatidylserine, or alpha-GPC) varies by manufacturer.

Cocoabuterol — branded cocoa extract (Theobroma cacao) standardized to naturally occurring alkaloids and catechins. Marketed for thermogenesis and beta-2 adrenergic stimulation. Identity mapping for proprietary label tokens.

Conjugated bile acids (glycocholic acid, taurocholic acid) — endogenous emulsifiers produced by liver. Used in digestive support supplements. Identity mapping for bile acid descriptor rows on supplement labels.

Calcium glycerophosphate — organic calcium salt providing both calcium and phosphorus. Used as mineral source and buffering agent. ~19% elemental calcium. Good bioavailability. Identity mapping pending form-level IQM curation.

Calcium caprylate — calcium salt of caprylic acid (C8). Medium-chain fatty acid salt used as antifungal support and calcium source. Identity mapping for caprylic acid salt labels on supplement formulations.

Crominex 3+ — branded trivalent chromium complex by Natreon Inc. containing chromium, Phyllanthus emblica, and Shilajit. Studied for blood glucose management. Identity mapping for proprietary chromium labels.

Beta-ecdysterone (20-hydroxyecdysone) — insect molting hormone found in spinach and Cyanotis vaga. Studied for anabolic/protein synthesis effects (doi:10.1007/s00204-019-02490-x). WADA monitoring list. Identity mapping for label tokens.
Identity-only mapping for generic carotene descriptor labels pending explicit isomer disclosure.

Malate — generic malic acid/salt descriptor on supplement labels. L-malic acid is a Krebs cycle intermediate. Used as acidulant, flavor enhancer, and mineral chelation acid. Identity mapping for unspecified malate forms.

ASU (Avocado Soybean Unsaponifiables) — lipid extract from avocado and soybean oils. Studied for osteoarthritis via cartilage metabolism modulation (doi:10.1136/ard.60.2.171). Soy allergen. French prescription status (Piascledine 300).

Alpha-glycosyl isoquercitrin (AGIQ, enzymatically modified isoquercitrin) — water-soluble quercetin glycoside with enhanced bioavailability. FDA GRAS (GRN 220). Antioxidant. Used as food additive and supplement ingredient in Japan (FOSHU).

AMP-XT — branded adenosine monophosphate or AMP-citrate stimulant complex. Identity mapping for proprietary energy/thermogenic supplement label tokens. Specific composition varies by manufacturer formulation.

Anti-Fungi 11x/88 — branded antifungal herbal complex descriptor (typically caprylic acid + oregano + undecylenic acid base). Identity mapping for proprietary formulation naming. Specific composition varies by manufacturer.

Anti-Fungi 10x/88 — branded antifungal herbal complex descriptor (typically caprylic acid + oregano base). Identity mapping for proprietary antifungal formulation naming. Specific composition varies by manufacturer.

99%+ purity CBD (cannabidiol) isolate from Cannabis sativa L. hemp. THC-free by definition (≤LOQ). Farm Bill 2018 compliant. Identity mapping for high-purity hemp isolate descriptors on supplement labels.
Generic descriptor for muscle tissue/muscle complex label rows (e.g., 'muscle tissue', 'cardiac muscle'). Non-discrete ingredient — mapped for identity routing of glandular tissue/organ label text.

L-Asparagine — non-essential amino acid synthesized from aspartate. Used in protein synthesis and nitrogen metabolism. Found in asparagus and many plant sources. Identity mapping pending dedicated amino acid IQM curation.

Oleamide (9-octadecenamide) — endogenous fatty acid amide involved in sleep induction. Found in cerebrospinal fluid. CB1 receptor modulator. Used in sleep/relaxation supplement formulations. Identity mapping for label tokens.

Phloridzin (phlorizin) — dihydrochalcone glycoside from apple tree bark. SGLT1/SGLT2 inhibitor — blocks renal glucose reabsorption. Historical antidiabetic compound; basis for modern gliflozin drugs. Standardization marker.

Total sulfur content descriptor on supplement labels — represents elemental sulfur from MSM, SAMe, cysteine, methionine, or other sulfur-containing compounds. Not a discrete ingredient; analytical specification marker.
Glandular/organ-derived identity mapping for aorta tissue terms. Identity-only mapping; no direct scoring bonus/penalty.
Glandular/organ-derived identity mapping for brain tissue terms. Identity-only mapping; no direct scoring bonus/penalty.
Glandular/organ-derived identity mapping for pineal tissue terms. Identity-only mapping; no direct scoring bonus/penalty.

Whole wheat (Triticum aestivum) bran — outer husk layer of wheat kernel. Provides insoluble dietary fiber (~43%). Non-scorable food ingredient. Gluten-containing — must declare per FALCPA. Used as filler/carrier.

Arrowroot starch (Maranta arundinacea) — easily digestible, hypoallergenic starch used as filler, carrier, and binding agent in supplement tablets. GRAS food ingredient. Gluten-free. Bland taste, good for taste masking.
Identity mapping for sodium caprylate terms; non-scorable unless safety policy changes.
Identity mapping for magnesium caprylate terms; non-scorable unless safety policy changes.
Identity mapping for ursolic/oleanolic acid descriptor terms; treated as non-scorable identity bucket pending dedicated scoring model.

Bovine bone marrow concentrate — used in traditional glandular/organ therapy formulations. Contains mesenchymal stem cell niche factors, collagen, and marrow fat. No controlled human efficacy data. USDA-inspected source.

Salmon (Oncorhynchus spp.) nasal cartilage — marine-derived source of proteoglycans (chondroitin sulfate) and type II collagen. Used in joint support formulations. Fish allergen — must declare per FALCPA.

Edible hemoglobin (typically bovine or porcine blood-derived) — used as bioavailable heme iron source in supplement formulations. Iron content ~0.34%. Mapped as non-scorable identity entry. USDA-inspected source.

Ground Salvia hispanica (chia) seed used as filler/binder in tablets and capsules. Rich in omega-3 ALA, fiber, and protein. GRAS as food ingredient; no known safety concerns at typical supplement excipient levels.

Ground Syzygium aromaticum (clove) flower buds used as flavoring agent. Contains eugenol (70-90% of essential oil). GRAS flavoring under 21 CFR 184.1257. Antimicrobial properties; no safety concerns at flavoring doses.

Natural fruit-juice-derived colorant from sienna (Cassia) fruit extract. Used as brown/amber plant-based colorant in supplement tablets and capsules. Clean-label alternative to synthetic iron oxide pigments.

Dehydrated bone broth (typically bovine) used as protein source and flavoring in supplement powders. Provides collagen peptides, glycine, and trace minerals. Not a recognized allergen; sourced from slow-simmered animal bones.
Natural cinnamon (Cinnamomum spp.) flavoring used for taste masking in chewable tablets and gummies. GRAS flavoring under 21 CFR 182.10. Contains cinnamaldehyde; safe at flavoring concentrations.

Native micellar casein protein isolated from milk via microfiltration. Used as slow-release protein source in supplement formulations. Major dairy allergen (milk protein); must be declared per FALCPA.

Hydrolyzed protein derived from fish (typically cod, pollock, or tilapia) skin/scales via enzymatic hydrolysis. Used as collagen source in supplement formulations. Fish allergen; must be declared per FALCPA.

Plant-derived colorants (typically carotenoids, anthocyanins, or curcuminoids) used for yellow/orange coloring in supplement tablets and capsules. Clean-label alternatives to synthetic dyes like FD&C Yellow No. 5/6.

TruMask is a proprietary flavor-masking technology by Sensient Technologies. Used as liquid or powder coating to reduce bitter/metallic taste of active ingredients in oral supplements. Inert carrier system; no active pharmacological effects.
Macadamia-derived oil used as a carrier lipid in softgels and specialty oil blends.
Pea-derived fiber used for bulking, texture, and fiber fortification in powder blends.

aliases: organic brown rice protein magnesium chelate
other_ingredients.notes: Rice-protein chelate delivery form used to bind and carry magnesium in whole-food style mineral blends.
Goat milk protein used as a dairy protein base in powders and specialty nutrition products.
Whole adrenal glandular tissue ingredient mapped for identity traceability. Treated as non-scorable unless a dedicated glandular quality model is added.
Vegetable carrier oil derived from rice bran. Used in softgels and lipid-based supplement delivery systems as an inert oil carrier.

Monoterpene from citrus peel oils. Used in supplement softgels and liquids as a flavoring or solvent/carrier component. In inactive rows, this route should take precedence over active-compound inference.
